# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

| Version | Supported          |
| ------- | ------------------ |
| 1.0.x   | :white_check_mark: |

## Reporting a Vulnerability

CIDX takes security issues very seriously. We appreciate your efforts to responsibly disclose your findings.

**DO NOT** file a public issue on GitHub for security vulnerabilities.

Please use one of these secure methods to report vulnerabilities:

1. Use GitHub's private vulnerability reporting feature by visiting [our Security page](https://github.com/CIDXMCP/cidx/security/advisories/new)
2. Send an email to [security@cidx.dev](mailto:security@cidx.dev) with details about the issue

**What to include in your report:**
- Type of issue (e.g. buffer overflow, SQL injection, or cross-site scripting)
- Full paths of source file(s) related to the manifestation of the issue
- The location of the affected source code (tag/branch/commit or direct URL)
- Any special configuration required to reproduce the issue
- Step-by-step instructions to reproduce the issue
- Proof-of-concept or exploit code (if possible)
- Impact of the issue, including how an attacker might exploit the issue

We will acknowledge receipt of your vulnerability report as soon as possible and will send you regular updates about our progress.

## Disclosure Policy

When we receive a security bug report, we will:
1. Confirm the problem and determine the affected versions
2. Audit code to find any potential similar problems
3. Prepare fixes for all releases still under maintenance
4. Release the fixes as soon as possible

## Comments on this Policy

If you have suggestions on how this process could be improved, please submit a pull request.
